screenshot_status: FAILED — Puppeteer could not launch browser (no dbus socket, thread resource limits in sandbox). Review conducted via full source code analysis only.

regression_check.bug_1_stats_bar_overflow: PARTIALLY FIXED — media queries added at 900px (3-col) and 600px (2-col) are good. BUT conditional 6th (error) and 7th (stuck) stat cards still overflow the 5-col grid template at any viewport width where both are visible. No overflow: hidden or grid wrapping safety net.
regression_check.bug_2_inter_font_not_loaded: NOT FIXED — index.css still declares font-family: 'Inter' with no @import or <link> in index.html. Inter silently falls back to Segoe UI / system-ui on all platforms.
regression_check.bug_3_mobile_worker_names_hidden: NOT FIXED — Office.css line 1147: .char__name { display: none } at @media (max-width: 480px). Worker names are completely invisible on phones. Made worse by a contradictory second 480px block at line 2318 that sets .char__name { font-size: 0.32rem } on an already-hidden element — dead CSS that implies the author tried to fix it but used the wrong property.
regression_check.bug_4_stuck_card_missing_a11y: NOT FIXED — stat-card--stuck (App.jsx lines 452–467) has onClick but zero tabIndex, zero role, zero aria-label. Keyboard-only users cannot reach or activate it.
regression_check.bug_5_task_tag_focus_visible: NOT FIXED — .task-tag has tabIndex={0} and role='button' in JSX but App.css has no .task-tag:focus-visible rule. Keyboard focus is invisible on task filter chips.
regression_check.bug_6_ping_aria_live: NOT FIXED — ping reaction text (PING_REACTIONS) fires into Character's speech bubble but no aria-live region announces the reaction to screen readers. Sighted users see it; blind users get nothing.
regression_check.bug_7_office_jsx_monolith: NOT FIXED — Office.jsx is now 1,483 lines. CharacterAvatar, Character, WindowElement, ConferenceTable, VibeShockwave, WallClock, Office all in one file. Still grows every review.
regression_check.bug_8_settimeout_leak_ping: NOT FIXED — handleCharClick line 1067: setTimeout(() => setPingReaction(null), 3000) has no ref and no cleanup. If Office unmounts while the timer runs, it fires a state update on an unmounted component tree.
regression_check.new_finding_vibe_weather_label_key_bug: NEW — VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS (Office.jsx line 109) uses key 'crushing-it' but getTeamVibeKey() returns 'crushing'. aria-label on .vibe-weather falls back to the bare key string for the most common vibe state.
regression_check.new_finding_duplicate_css_office_floor_v: NEW — .office-floor[data-vibe=...] rules appear TWICE in Office.css (lines 91–95 without quotes, then 97–114 with quotes). The duplicated declarations add ~120 lines of dead CSS and risk specificity confusion.
regression_check.new_finding_constants_inside_component: ONGOING — IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES inside Character() body; FULL_IDLE_MESSAGES inside Office() body. These are recreated on every render. Minor perf waste but signals habitually unhoisted constants.

suggestions.title: [4+ REVIEWS] Load Inter font or remove the declaration
suggestions.description: src/index.css declares font-family: 'Inter', ... but no @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap') exists and index.html has no <link> for it. Either add the Google Fonts link tag to index.html, import via CSS, or change the font-family to start with 'Segoe UI' and drop the Inter pretense. This has been open since at least Review #27.

suggestions.title: [4+ REVIEWS] Mobile worker names hidden at ≤480px
suggestions.description: Office.css @media (max-width: 480px) sets .char__name { display: none }. Names are critical for identity on the office floor — hiding them makes the visualization useless on phones. Fix: change to font-size: 0.3rem with overflow: hidden and max-width on the char__name element. Note: there is ALSO a second contradictory @media (max-width: 480px) block at line 2318 that sets .char__name { font-size: 0.32rem } on an already-hidden element — delete the dead rule.

suggestions.title: [4+ REVIEWS] Stats bar overflows with 6th/7th conditional cards
suggestions.description: stats-bar uses grid-template-columns: repeat(5, 1fr) but the error card and stuck card can add a 6th and 7th item. These auto-flow onto new rows, breaking the bar layout. Fix: change to grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(180px, 1fr)) which handles any number of cards. Also add a 1-col fallback at max-width: 380px since the current smallest breakpoint (600px) still yields 2 cramped columns on the narrowest phones.

suggestions.title: [4+ REVIEWS] Stuck worker card is keyboard-inaccessible
suggestions.description: stat-card--stuck in App.jsx (lines 452–467) has an onClick handler but no tabIndex, role, or aria-label. Add tabIndex={0}, role='button', aria-label that describes the stuck worker, and onKeyDown for Enter/Space. Also add .stat-card--stuck:focus-visible to App.css.

suggestions.title: [4+ REVIEWS] Task tag filter chips have no :focus-visible style
suggestions.description: Task tags have tabIndex={0} and role='button' (App.jsx line 297-298) but App.css has no .task-tag:focus-visible rule. Keyboard users cannot see focus. Add: .task-tag:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid currentColor; outline-offset: 2px; } to App.css.

suggestions.title: [4+ REVIEWS] Ping reaction speech bubble has no aria-live announcement
suggestions.description: When a character is pinged, PING_REACTIONS text appears in a speech bubble (Character component). No aria-live region announces this to screen readers. Fix: add a visually hidden <div aria-live='polite' aria-atomic='true' className='sr-only'> in Office that receives pingReaction text when it changes. This gives screen reader users equivalent feedback to the visual bubble.

suggestions.title: [4+ REVIEWS] setTimeout leak in ping reaction handler
suggestions.description: In Office.jsx handleCharClick (line 1067): setTimeout(() => setPingReaction(null), 3000) is not stored in a ref and has no cleanup. If the Office component unmounts while this timer is live, it attempts to setState on an unmounted component. Fix: add a pingReactionTimerRef = useRef(null), store the timer ID there, clear it in a useEffect cleanup, and clear it at the top of handleCharClick like pingTimerRef already does.

suggestions.title: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS key mismatch — 'crushing-it' vs 'crushing'
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 109: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS uses key 'crushing-it' but getTeamVibeKey() returns 'crushing' (confirmed in vibe.js). The aria-label on .vibe-weather for the crushing vibe falls back to the bare string 'crushing' instead of the descriptive '☀️ Clear skies — team at full strength'. Fix: change 'crushing-it' to 'crushing' in VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS.

suggestions.title: Duplicate .office-floor[data-vibe=...] CSS rules in Office.css
suggestions.description: Office.css defines .office-floor[data-vibe=...] twice: lines 91–95 (without quotes on attribute value) and lines 97–114 (with quotes). The second set overrides the first entirely. Delete lines 91–95 (the un-quoted set) to remove ~5 duplicate declarations and eliminate potential specificity ambiguity. Net: removes ~120 chars of dead CSS.

suggestions.title: Dead CSS in second @media (max-width: 480px) block — .char__name
suggestions.description: Office.css has two separate @media (max-width: 480px) blocks. The first (line 1133) sets .char__name { display: none }. The second (line 2318) sets .char__name { font-size: 0.32rem } on the now-invisible element. The font-size rule is unreachable dead code. Either fix bug #2 (restore display) and keep the font-size, or delete the dead font-size rule. Merge the two 480px blocks while here.

suggestions.title: Hoist component-level constants out of render functions
suggestions.description: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS and ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES are declared inside the Character component body (lines 482, 498). FULL_IDLE_MESSAGES is declared inside Office() (line 1038). These objects are recreated on every render. Move them to module scope above the component definitions (alongside AWAY_MESSAGES, PING_REACTIONS, etc.) to make intent clear and avoid per-render allocation.

suggestions.title: Split Office.jsx monolith — 1,483 lines and growing
suggestions.description: Office.jsx has grown to 1,483 lines across 8+ distinct components (CharacterAvatar, Character, WindowElement, ConferenceTable, VibeShockwave, WallClock, Office, helper functions). Suggested split: (1) src/office/CharacterAvatar.jsx — SVG character sprites; (2) src/office/Character.jsx — animated character wrapper; (3) src/office/ConferenceTable.jsx + WallClock.jsx + WindowElement.jsx — furniture; (4) src/office/Office.jsx — main layout only. This will also improve hot-reload performance.

suggestions.title: Ghost character (OOO) variant needs aria-label describing their absence
suggestions.description: Ghost workers render as characters with variant='ghost' and tabIndex={0}, but no role. Without a role, a screen reader announces this as a generic focusable div. Add role='img' and aria-label='[Name], out of office — [role]' to the ghost variant's wrapping div in Character. The ghost-ooo-bubble tooltip text is present but not announced on focus.

suggestions.title: char-hover-card overflows viewport on rightmost desk characters
suggestions.description: The .char-hover-card uses position: absolute with no boundary awareness. Characters near the right edge (desks at left >70%) will have their hover card clipped or overflow the office-floor container. Add a CSS check or JS-based positioning to flip the card leftward when near the right edge. Quick CSS fix: add left: auto; right: 0 for chars with a data-desk-position='right' attribute.

suggestions.title: Inline styles on ambient particles and lone-survivor-label should be CSS classes
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1387 and 1469–1476 use inline style objects with animation properties for lone-survivor-label and ambient-particles spans. React recreates these objects on every render. Move to CSS classes / CSS custom properties to avoid the allocation.

suggestions.title: Plant toast notification missing role='status'
suggestions.description: The ficus level-up toast (Office.jsx lines 1136–1160) uses aria-live='polite' which is correct, but also has pointerEvents: none. Adding role='status' alongside aria-live makes the live region semantics explicit per ARIA best practices.

suggestions.title: Remove VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS and VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS from Office.jsx — move to constants.js
suggestions.description: Office.jsx defines VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS (line 108) and VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS (line 115) locally but they are pure static data with no component coupling. Move them to src/utils/constants.js alongside ROLE_COLORS, STATUS_LABELS, etc., for a single source of truth and easier testability.

suggestions.title: Desk SVG monitor screens lack aria-label for keyboard users inspecting desks
suggestions.description: Each desk has a DESK_SCREEN_SVG that shows a character's work context (code editor, bar chart, etc.) but all have aria-hidden='true'. Consider adding an aria-label to the .desk-monitor-screen that describes what's on screen (e.g., 'Carlos's screen showing code') — it adds texture for AT users who can navigate the desk area.

what_was_fixed_since_review_30: NOTHING. All 8 flagged bugs from Review #30 remain open. The stats bar responsive breakpoints (900px, 600px) were already present — not a new fix. No accessibility improvements were shipped. No code quality refactoring was done.
summary: QA Review #31 — Code review only (Puppeteer could not launch in sandbox environment). This is the fifth consecutive review where the same three HIGH-priority layout/font bugs remain unaddressed. The 'Inter font not loaded' bug is a one-line fix that has been open since Review #27. Worker names disappearing on phones — a real usability regression — was in Review #28's bug list. The stuck worker card, task tag focus styles, and ping aria-live are accessibility debt that has compounded across reviews. On the positive side: the codebase is genuinely delightful. The vibe system, animated characters, PANIC screens, ficus growth, wall clock, and conference table huddles show real creative energy. The code is readable and the React patterns (useMemo, memo, useRef for timers) are mostly sound. But the gap between feature ambition and hygiene discipline is widening. Score: 3.4/5. Would be 4.2 if the three one-liner bugs (font, names, stuck card tabIndex) were simply addressed.
